description:
GOAL: Resolve `Term::TailCallClosure` and `Term::CallClosure` whose closure
operand is bound to a `MakeClosure` in the same block before the terminator.
Rewrite to TailCall/Call to the underlying lambda fn so .15.2/.15.3 can splice.

Parent: fz-ul4.11.15. Depends on: fz-ul4.11.15.3.

## Deliverables (extend src/ir_inline.rs)

  /// One pass over the module. For each block whose terminator is
  /// TailCallClosure(cv, args) or CallClosure(cv, args, cont), scan the
  /// block's stmts in order:
  ///   - If we encounter Let(cv, MakeClosure(F, captured)), record (F,
  ///     captured) as the resolved closure.
  ///   - If we encounter Let(cv, _other_) AFTER recording, INVALIDATE the
  ///     resolution (cv has been reassigned).
  /// If a resolution is still valid at end-of-block, rewrite the terminator:
  ///   TailCallClosure(cv, args)
  ///     -> TailCall { callee: F, args: [captured..., args...] }
  ///   CallClosure(cv, args, cont)
  ///     -> Call { callee: F, args: [captured..., args...], continuation: cont }
  /// Returns count of rewrites.
  pub fn resolve_known_closures_once(m: &mut Module) -> usize;

## Lambda argument convention

  Per ir_lower.rs:586-592, a lambda fn F's entry params are:
      [captured_0, ..., captured_M, arg_0, ..., arg_N]
  So the rewritten TailCall/Call arg list is `captured ++ args` in that order.

## Why same-block-only

  CPS-split breaks blocks at every Call/TailCall/CallClosure/TailCallClosure.
  Any code BEFORE such a term lives in the same block; any code AFTER lives
  in the continuation fn (a different FnIr). Therefore "same block, preceding
  the terminator" is the maximally reachable scope without cross-fn dataflow.

  Cross-block / cross-fn closure tracing (e.g., closure passed through a fn
  param, or built in an earlier block) requires flow analysis — out of scope
  for v0. Tracked as a follow-up consideration in fz-ul4.11.15 parent
  description.

## Reassignment detection

  Vars in fz-IR are SSA-shaped (FnBuilder mints fresh Vars per let), so
  reassignment of `cv` is extremely rare in lowered output. The defensive
  invalidation guard exists to handle hand-built IRs (in tests) and any future
  pass that might violate SSA.

## Composition

  This pass produces TailCall/Call terms but does NOT splice. Splicing is
  done by inline_tail_calls_once / inline_calls_once on the next round. The
  fixpoint driver in .15.5 orders the passes:
    repeat:
      n = resolve_known_closures_once(m)
      n += inline_calls_once(m)
      n += inline_tail_calls_once(m)
      stop if n == 0 or rounds >= MAX_ITERATIONS

  This ordering lets a single MakeClosure → resolve → splice complete in two
  rounds.

## Tests

  - Closure literal at call site (closure built earlier in same block):
      let cv = closure(F, [a])
      tail_call_closure cv [b]
    rewrites to `tail_call F [a, b]`.
  - Same for `CallClosure(cv, [b], cont)` -> `Call(F, [a, b], cont)`.
  - Closure from block param: cv is a param of the block (not bound by a
    MakeClosure in this block) -> NOT rewritten.
  - Closure bound but then reassigned later in same block -> NOT rewritten.
  - Closure bound via MakeClosure in a DIFFERENT block -> NOT rewritten.
  - ir_interp parity: a small fn `apply(f, x) = f(x)` lowered with a
    closure literal — pre- and post-rewrite produce same value.

## Acceptance

  - cargo test passes.
  - One commit referencing fz-ul4.11.15.4.

## References

  - fz-ul4.11.15 architecture, Cases 3-4.
  - ir_lower.rs:574-627 (MakeClosure / lambda construction).
  - ir_interp.rs:140-166 (closure call semantics).
title: Inline closure-call sites (Cases 3-4)
